data.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 59ef "Verified fake: contract tests run against BOTH real and fake AcliClient"), cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/822a/8937/fe57) and corroborated against the repo. Each of the 7 cross-child interaction modes was checked; NO high-confidence finding is attributable to this pairing. Clean pairing (empty findings).

Repo corroboration: the enrichment surface 59ef puts under contract — get_comment_map (acli_graph.py L289), get_issuelinks_map (L391), get_parent_map, search_issues — all exist on the real AcliClient and return the raw REST-nested field shapes (comment field dict {"comments":[...]}; issuelinks list with type.name + inward/outwardIssue), exactly what fetcher.fetch_snapshot merges (fetcher.py L413-417, L450-454). The shapes 59ef asserts are real, and the methods are not moved/renamed by any sibling.

7-mode walk for THIS pairing:
- Implicit shared state — BENIGN. 59ef depends_on fe57 and exercises fe57's FakeAcliClient (tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/_fakes.py) plus the real AcliClient (acli_graph.py). fe57 BUILDS the fake; 59ef VERIFIES it. Clean producer/verifier split with a declared dependency edge; no conflicting writes to a shared file (59ef adds test_acliclient_contract.py and only reads acli_graph.py).
- Conflicting assumptions — BENIGN. fe57 AC2 ("FakeAcliClient serves fixtures through the production fetch_snapshot enrichment with NO shape transformation") and 59ef AC2 ("the fake FAILS the contract if its return shape diverges from the real client's") are mutually reinforcing, not contradictory: a fixture-faithful, unmassaged fake is precisely what passes 59ef's shape contract. No count-vs-list / representation mismatch on the inbound enrichment surface (unlike the outbound labels_applied issue on 2359/33d0, which 59ef does not touch).
- Dependency gap — BENIGN. 59ef correctly declares depends_on fe57 (needs the fake to run the hermetic leg). It correctly scopes the snapshot round-trip OUT (→ 8937) and fixture capture OUT (→ fe57); it needs neither 8937's schema nor 822a's probe work to stand up its dual-run harness. No missing input edge.
- Scope overlap — BENIGN. 59ef asserts the enrichment METHODS' return shape (client surface); 8937 asserts the assembled SNAPSHOT-ENTRY schema after fetch; 822a asserts inbound↔outbound ROUND-TRIP behavior. Three distinct surfaces exercising fidelity from different angles — complementary, not duplicative.
- Ordering violation — BENIGN. The only ordering constraint (fake before its verifier) is captured by the declared depends_on fe57. No unsequenced same-region co-edit (contrast the 33d0↔2359 outbound-applier shared-state cluster, which involves neither 59ef's files nor its surface).
- Consumer impact — BENIGN. The dual-run harness adds a live-gated leg; 59ef AC4 makes live opt-in and explicitly guards against flaking/slowing the default suite (consistent with parent AC7 "no net increase in brittle tests"), and AC3 asserts structure/type not exact values, so it does not impose brittle expectations on any consumer.
- Residual references — BENIGN. All four referenced methods exist in acli_graph.py; the referenced fake is the artifact fe57 produces. 33d0 collapses the OUTBOUND applier paths (apply_outbound.py/batch_dispatch.py), not the INBOUND enrichment methods 59ef contracts, so 33d0's deletion strands nothing here. No dangling reference.

The roster-level parent-AC6 absence (documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNING new tests) is a G3 coverage matter already tracked across the sibling set and on this ticket's prior G3 audits — it is not a G4 cross-child inconsistency, so it is not re-filed here.

VERDICT for this pairing (parent × 59ef): no G4 cross-child finding; all 7 interaction modes read benign under the high-confidence/benign-reading filter.
